channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Intermittent fasting doesn't have an edge for weight loss, but might still work for some, research shows
item.description: Intermittent fasting has become a buzzword in nutrition circles, with many people looking to it as a way to lose weight or improve their health.

item.title: The postpartum experience: Recognizing complications
item.description: Postpartum experiences are as unique as the pregnancies that come before them. Because of this, it can be difficult to recognize what's normal and wha...

item.title: Asking what love would do transforms leadership [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Pediatrician and certified coach Jessie Mahoney discusses her article...

item.title: A huge study finds a link between cannabis use in teens and psychosis later
item.description: Researchers followed more than 400,000 teens until they were adults. It found that those who used marijuana were more likely to develop serious mental...

item.title: Stop picking at your cuticles! 7 ways to keep your nails healthy and strong
item.description: Should you trim your cuticles? How do you cut a hangnail? Is it better to use a cardboard or crystal file? Dermatologists and a nail technician share ...

item.title: Poly-L-lysine-coated catheters are safe and well-tolerated in adults requiring short-term catheterization
item.description: 1. Poly-L-lysine (PLL)-coated catheters are well-tolerated, safe, and noninferior in preventing bacteriuria compared to noble metal alloys (NMA)-coate...

item.title: Peptides for chronic pain: Navigating safety and regulations

item.title: Agentic AI: the key to saving annual preventive exams
item.description: Annual preventive exams have been a staple of American medicine for decades. Yet despite their importance, preventive visits remain underutilized in A...

item.title: Watching the 2026 Winter Olympics? Here is why athletes treat danger differently

item.title: Integrative oncology nutrition: a case study in leukemia recovery

item.title: Mifepristone safety: Comparing the data to Viagra and penicillin
item.description: As physicians, we pride ourselves on following the evidence. We weigh risks and benefits, assess data, and trust science to guide decisions about pati...

item.title: AI reads clinical notes to forecast colitis-linked colorectal cancer
item.description: People with ulcerative colitis (UC), a chronic inflammatory bowel disease, are up to four times more likely to develop colorectal cancer than the gene...

item.title: Brain injury is almost ten times more common in unhoused people. Addressing it is key to reducing homelessness
item.description: On any given night, 60,000 people in Canada will go to sleep homeless. Research estimates that more than half of them have had a brain injury at one p...

item.title: Facing a mental health crisis, an NJ school pulled a beloved novel from English class
item.description: Junot DÃ­az's The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao was removed from an English class at the public school. PEN America says it's part of a trend of scr...

item.title: For years the Taliban told women to cover up in public. Now they're cracking down
item.description: At hospitals, at seminaries and on buses, the Taliban is stepping up enforcement of rules on women's dress in the city of Herat.

item.title: Penpulimab and lenalidomide in combination with standard of care chemoimmunotherapy demonstrates promising safety and efficacy in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma
item.description: 1. Penpulimab and lenalidomide in combination with rituximab, gemcitabine, and oxaliplatin (R-GemOx regimen) is safe, tolerable, and shows antitumour ...

item.title: Artificial intelligence predicts colorectal cancer in ulcerative colitis with 99% accuracy
item.description: 1. A new AI workflow utilizing large language models can predict with 99% accuracy which ulcerative colitis patients will remain free of colorectal ca...

item.title: Spartanburg measles outbreak: How did we get here?
item.description: South Carolina is seeing the biggest measles outbreak in the U.S. in decades, spreading mainly among unvaccinated children. At the epicenter is a stor...

item.title: Healthy Life Expectancy Declines Again
item.description: Years spent in good health have dropped to their lowest level since comparable data were first collected in 2011 to 2013, with stark regional gaps acr...

item.title: Certain Occupations Linked to Higher IBD Risk
item.description: According to a large international study, specific occupations were linked to higher IBD risk, with diesel exposure, work patterns, and socioeconomic ...

item.title: Protective Clothing, Low-Cost Sunscreens May Cut Expenses
item.description: The annual cost of recommended sunscreen use varies by up to 36-fold, depending on unit price and concurrent sun-protective behaviors. Medscape Medica...

item.title: Birthing Techniques Linked to ADHD Risk
item.description: Using a vacuum followed by forceps was associated with a higher risk for ADHD than delivery by second-stage cesarean. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Artificial intelligence designed drugs Hit 90% Phase I success rate in trials
